page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, ensuring dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading. The memory configuration provides 384.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB per core. For local storage, the instance includes 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps, managed via the Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier results in multi-core stress-ng tests and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only small block speeds reaching 13843.78 GB/sec and uncached large block reads at 365.15 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET operations also rank in the top tier, with Redis handling 268.05 million operations per second. Conversely, single-core performance and cryptographic operations sit in the average tier.

Given its ARM64 architecture and high resource density, the m9gd.24xlarge offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for multi-threaded workloads compared to traditional x86 architectures. The combination of 96 dedicated cores, 384.0 GB of RAM, and 5700 GB of fast local NVMe storage makes this instance highly suitable for high-throughput database operations, static web serving, large-scale multi-threaded data processing, and medium-sized language model inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processors. It delivers 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of RAM and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. Benchmark results highlight top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, exceptional memory bandwidth, and outstanding database performance, as demonstrated by its top-tier Redis SET operations and multi-threaded decompression speeds. While single-core and cryptographic performance remain average, its high core density and integrated local storage offer excellent qualitative cost efficiency for resource-intensive workloads. This server is highly suited for high-throughput database hosting, static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server featuring 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.24xlarge is an ARM64 server with 96 dedicated Graviton cores, 384 GB RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density ARM64 general-purpose platform featuring dedicated physical cores, massive local NVMe storage, and top-tier multi-threaded performance.
bullet_points: AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V3 processor with 96 dedicated physical cores
384.0 GB of system memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and Redis database performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the Nitro hypervisor
Well-suited for high-throughput databases, web serving, and multi-threaded data processing
